figures.caption: Figure 1: Sketch of the affine subspace extension in a simplified three-dimensional setting. The contour lines of π̄∆ (dashed lines) are depicted in a plane parallel to the ξ2-ξ3-plane (dotted rectangle). The initial affine subspace is here represented as a straight line in ξ1-direction. At the minimum of the potential π̄∆ along that line, the gradient ∂π̄∆/∂ξ serves as new direction for the affine subspace extension, which would in this case correspond to the plane spanned by the gradient and the initial affine subspace. Subsequently, the minimum within this plane is sought for.
Figure 2: a) Hardening curves for the different parameter sets in Tab. 1 and for the material introduced in Tab. 2. b) Enlarged view of the smaller yield stress region.

Figure 3: a) to c) RMS-error for three different materials, highlighting the decrease in RMS-error with an increasing number of modes, generalized IPs, and training simulations. d) Comparison of the different materials for 202 training simulations, demonstrating the impact of material parameters on the accuracy of E3C results.
Figure 4: Dimensions, mesh and boundary conditions of the porous mounting plate.
Figure 5: Two-scale simulation results with a macroscopic deformation scale factor of 5 (the microscopic deformations are unscaled).
Figure 6: Comparison of E3C predictions (full lines) with FEM (dashed lines) for microstructures 1)-6) in Fig. 5. The shear stresses/strains are given in Mandel notation (σ̄∗ ij = √ 2/2σ̄ij).

Figure 7: Left: comparison of the reaction force for the different formulations. Right: von Mises equivalent stress plotted along the green line (upper right picture) for the four states illustrated by the squares in the left diagram.

Figure 8: a) Online effort, as described by the Tab. 3. Starting with 4 IPs at the macroscale and the conventional E3C hyper-reduction on the microscale (red column) the online effort can be decreased with ’Sequential Subspace Mode Adaptation’ (orange column). Additional application of the mean strain element (yellow column) leads to online speeds even closer to single scale(green column). b) Exemplary offline effort for 210 and 49 snapshot simulations.

Table 4: Relative residual norms of macroscopic Newton iterations for different time steps for the model with Sequential Subspace Mode Adaptation. The blue residuals mark the Newton iteration used for the adaptation of the modes.

Figure 9: Comparison of E3C predictions (full lines) with FEM (dashed lines) for particle-reinforced microstructures 1)-6). The shear stresses/strains are given in Mandel notation (σ̄∗ ij = √ 2/2σ̄ij).
